clusters.title: Gunshots heard near White House
clusters.neutral_headline: Gunshots heard near White House
bullet_summary: Reported by The Age, 9News, ABC News Australia

items.title: Gunshots heard near White House
items.standfirst: Law enforcement is responding after apparent gunshots were heard near the White House.
items.content: Law enforcement is responding after apparent gunshots were heard near the White House.

items.title: Reporter scrambles as gunshots heard near White House
items.standfirst: ABC US reporter Selina Wang was recording a video for social media when gunshots erupted near the White House.
items.content: ABC US reporter Selina Wang was recording a video for social media when gunshots erupted near the White House.

items.title: Shots fired near White House
items.standfirst: Reporter Selina Wang ducks for cover on the White House North Lawn after hearing gunshots fired nearby.
items.content: Reporter Selina Wang ducks for cover on the White House North Lawn after hearing gunshots fired nearby.

items.title: Two wounded in Secret Service shooting near White House
items.standfirst: O ne US reporter said it sounded like "dozens of gunshots" were fired in a video posted to X.
items.content: Two people have been shot and wounded by US Secret Service agents near the White House, which was briefly locked down due to the nearby gunfire this morning.A law enforcement official told CNN officers from the Secret Service's uniformed division responded to a report of a person firing a weapon when the incident took place. Trump is currently in residence at the White House, which was briefly locked down.READ MORE: Man shot dead by police in Queensland in alleged DV incidenthttps://x.com/selinawangtv/status/2058312115098697760 That lockdown has since been lifted, CNN has reported. Journalists reporting on US President Donald Trump's claim of a nearly finalised peace deal with Iran were rushed to the briefing room and told to shelter in place after the noises were heard."I was in the middle of taping on my iPhone for a social video from the White House North Lawn when we heard the shots," ABC US reporter Selina Wang posted on X."It sounded like dozens of gunshots. We were told to sprint to the press briefing room where we are holding now. "A Secret Service official told CNN that the agency was investigating reports of shots fired at the corner of 17th Street and Pennsylvania Avenue Northwest, which is just outside the White House complex.READ MORE: Irish tradie issues warning to expats after shock diagnosis Secret Service agents carrying rifles could be seen moving through the North Lawn area following the incident and blocking the White House press briefing room.The Secret Service said it was "aware of reports of shots fired near 17th Street and Pennsylvania Avenue NW" — one block from the White House — and was "working to corroborate the information with personnel on the ground".In a social media post, FBI Director Kash Patel said officers were responding to shots fired and said he would "update the public as we're able."The lockdown comes a month after the White House Correspondents' Dinner, where reporters and Trump administration officials ducked for cover as shots rang out.The suspect in that shooting, Cole Tomas Allen, sprinted through a security checkpoint with a shotgun in hand, exchanging fire with Secret Service agents who chased behind him, according to security footage.He has pleaded not guilty to attempting to assassinate Trump and to other charges.More to come. – with CNN and Associated Press

items.title: Breaking: Suspect taken to hospital after gunshots send White House into lockdown
items.standfirst: Reporters were told to sprint into the Press Briefing Room after gunshots were heard nearby.
items.content: Reporters were told to sprint into the Press Briefing Room after gunshots were heard nearby.

items.title: Man shot dead by police in Queensland in alleged DV incident
items.standfirst: P olice say the man confronted the officers while carrying a firearm at a house in the City of Moreton Bay.
items.content: P olice have shot a man dead in the City of Moreton Bay in Queensland overnight.At about 12.30am, police were called to an alleged domestic violence incident at a house on Cavill Way in Narangba.There had been reports an armed man had been making threats.READ MORE: New footage emerges after Sydney underworld figure shot dead in Vietnam The man approached officers and threatened them while carrying a gun.Police say they shot the man in response. Officers provided first aid and CPR but the man died at the scene.Nobody else was injured and police say there is no ongoing risk to the community.An investigation into the shooting is underway.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

clusters.title: Multiple safety violations in China mine explosion, investigation finds
clusters.neutral_headline: Multiple safety violations in China mine explosion, investigation finds
bullet_summary: An explosion at a coal mine in China that killed at least 82 people was the result of the company involved having committed multiple serious safety violations, an early investigation has found
A gas explosion at a coal mine in China’s northern province of Shanxi killed at least 82 people, state media reported Saturday and after dozens were said to be trapped underground

items.title: Multiple safety violations in China mine explosion, investigation finds
items.standfirst: An explosion at a coal mine in China that killed at least 82 people was the result of the company involved having committed multiple serious safety violations, an early investigation has found.
items.content: An explosion at a coal mine in China that killed at least 82 people was the result of the company involved having committed multiple serious safety violations, an early investigation has found.

items.title: At least 90 killed in China coal mine gas explosion, dozens trapped
items.standfirst: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China’s northern province of Shanxi killed at least 82 people, state media reported Saturday and after dozens were said to be trapped underground.
items.content: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China's northern province of Shanxi has killed at least 90 people, state media says.Official news agency Xinhua said the accident at Changzhi city's Liushenyu coal mine happened on Friday evening. About 247 workers were on duty at the time.The agency initially reported early on Saturday that eight people were killed and 38 were trapped underground. Nine were still trapped as of Saturday afternoon, Xinhua said.READ MORE: Australian crime boss shot dead in VietnamREAD MORE: Driver in fiery crash minutes after police visit The cause of the explosion was under investigation, Xinhua reported, and rescue work was pressing on. Among the injured, many were hurt by toxic gas, according to state media CCTV.Chinese President Xi Jinping has called for an all-out effort to rescue the missing and an investigation of the accident's cause while holding those responsible accountable, reported Xinhua.READ MORE: Trump posts AI video of himself attacking Stephen ColbertShanxi province is known as China's main coal mining province.With a size larger than Greece and a population of about 34 million, the province's hundreds of thousands of miners dug 1.17 billion tonnes of coal last year, almost a third of China's total.
